response: The internet began as a US Department of Defense networking project in the 1960s, with ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969 by sending a message from UCLA to the Stanford Research Institute. A major turning point came in 1983 with the adoption of TCP/IP, and in 1989 Tim Berners-Lee created the World Wide Web, introducing HTML, URLs, and HTTP; Mosaic’s 1993 launch then helped bring the web to ordinary users. The text argues that the internet evolved from a research network into a mass global platform through milestones like the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash, the rise of social media in the mid-2000s, smartphones after the iPhone’s 2007 launch, and cloud computing with AWS in 2006. Today, the internet connects more than 5 billion people and underpins modern commerce, communication, and entertainment.
